paper_metadata.title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
paper_metadata.abstract: Transformers-based models, such as BERT, have been one of the most successful deep learning models for NLP. Unfortunately, one of their core limitations is the quadratic dependency (mainly in terms of memory) on the sequence length due to their full attention mechanism. To remedy this, we propose, BigBird, a sparse attention mechanism that reduces this quadratic dependency to linear. We show that BigBird is a universal approximator of sequence functions and is Turing complete, thereby preserving these properties of the quadratic, full attention model. Along the way, our theoretical analysis reveals some of the benefits of having $O(1)$ global tokens (such as CLS), that attend to the entire sequence as part of the sparse attention mechanism. The proposed sparse attention can handle sequences of length up to 8x of what was previously possible using similar hardware. As a consequence of the capability to handle longer context, BigBird drastically improves performance on various NLP tasks such as question answering and summarization. We also propose novel applications to genomics data.

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces a sparse attention mechanism based on a star graph and global tokens, enabling efficient modeling of long sequences while maintaining universal approximation and Turing completeness. This allows the model to handle longer input sequences effectively compared to standard Transformers.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Not explicitly stated in the provided text
extraction_data.extension: Uses a combination of global, windowed, and random attention heads to enable efficient long-range modeling, reducing quadratic complexity of full attention.
extraction_data.research_problem: Long sequence modeling in natural language processing
extraction_data.application: Question Answering (TriviaQA, HotpotQA, WikiHop), Document Classification (IMDb, Yelp-5, Arxiv, Patents, Hyperpartisan), GLUE benchmark tasks
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: BigBird introduces a sparse attention mechanism with global tokens, local tokens, and random tokens, enabling efficient modeling of long sequences while maintaining quadratic complexity only on a small subset of tokens. This allows the model to handle longer sequences effectively compared to standard Transformers.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Human reference genome (GRCh37), Saccharomyces Genome Database
extraction_data.research_problem: Long sequence modeling in natural language processing and genomics
extraction_data.application: Long document classification, question answering, promoter region prediction, chromatin-profile prediction
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces Big Bird, a sparse attention mechanism that enables efficient modeling of long sequences by combining global, windowed, and random attention patterns. This allows the model to scale to sequences up to 4096 tokens while maintaining performance comparable to full attention models.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Human reference GRCh37 (genomic DNA), converted into documents with sentences of 500–1000 base pairs; tokenized using SentencePiece with 32k vocabulary
extraction_data.application: Text summarization, genomic sequence analysis, long-document understanding
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM), Next Sentence Prediction (NSP)
extraction_data.extension: Uses a combination of global, windowed, and random attention heads to enable efficient long-range modeling, reducing quadratic complexity of full attention.
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
